status.source: EirGrid System Data Qtr Hourly spreadsheet
status.caveat: Best-effort parser. Thermal/other is not pure gas.

smartgrid_live.source: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard public pages
smartgrid_live.caveat: Smart Grid visible-page parser is disabled for demand, wind and solar because visible-page scraping can confuse actual, forecast and chart values. Core grid quantities use the official EirGrid quarter-hourly workbook instead.

carbon_intensity.caveat: Parsed from Smart Grid Dashboard chart API. ROI is preferred; ALL is used only as fallback and labelled.

smartgrid_api_live.caveat: Uses latest non-null actual rows at or before Ireland local time. Future null rows are rejected.

status.caveat: Best-effort parser. If SEAI blocks scripted access, the site uses an embedded official fallback snapshot and labels it as such.
Market/system prices are not household tariffs. Household affordability remains the SEAI semi-annual price layer.
sources: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard imbalance settlement price

status.caveat: Embedded official annual values; automate extraction later if source format remains stable.

daily_history_meta.caveat: Some early rows may be estimated warm-start rows for sparklines. Rows with estimated_backfill=true are visual placeholders only, not observed historical data. As real daily snapshots accumulate, estimated rows age out of the 30-day display window.

electricity.public_caveat: Core grid values are treated as live because the mapped source interval is within two hours.

recommended_next_source.name: Smart Grid Dashboard chart/API endpoint
recommended_next_source.reason: Needed for reliable live demand, wind, solar, generation, interconnection and fuel-mix values without visible-page scraping.

robust_api_fallback.name: ENTSO-E Transparency Platform
robust_api_fallback.note: Requires API token and correct Irish bidding-zone/control-area mapping.

items.label: Electricity system price
items.source: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard imbalance settlement price
items.freshness: latest settlement period visible on Smart Grid Dashboard
items.public_caveat: System/market signal, not a household tariff.

items.label: Gas balancing price
items.freshness: manually entered from Gas Networks Ireland table/export
items.public_caveat: System/market signal, not a household tariff.

do_not_do: Do not call stale workbook values live.
Do not mix dashboard percentages with workbook MW values without explicit denominator notes.
Do not treat thermal/other as measured gas.
Do not substitute household tariffs for market/system prices.

electricity_source_model.public_caveat: Core grid values are treated as live because the mapped source interval is within two hours.

recommended_next_source.name: Smart Grid Dashboard chart/API endpoint
recommended_next_source.reason: Needed for reliable live demand, wind, solar, generation, interconnection and fuel-mix values without visible-page scraping.

robust_api_fallback.name: ENTSO-E Transparency Platform
robust_api_fallback.note: Requires API token and correct Irish bidding-zone/control-area mapping.

electricity_now.renewables_definition: Latest non-null Smart Grid Dashboard API wind plus solar cover of current demand.
electricity_now.source_freshness: live chart API latest non-null actual
electricity_now.interconnection_note: Live SmartGrid System Generation page NET IMPORT % converted to MW using visible system generation.

daily_story.headline: Ireland remains thermal/other-backed in the latest live grid pulse.
daily_story.interpretation: This panel now uses Smart Grid Dashboard API live values for demand, wind and solar. The uncovered share is a computed remainder. It is not measured gas and does not yet split hydro, storage, fossil generation or imports because interconnection and full fuel mix are not yet mapped.

truth_meter.name: Renewable electricity
truth_meter.rule: 2030 benchmark is 80%; current gap is 38.7 pp.
truth_meter.why: This is the core 2030 electricity-transition indicator.
truth_meter.logic: Recent gain is 1.95 pp/yr versus 6.45 pp/yr required to 2030.

truth_meter.name: Thermal/other
truth_meter.rule: On track ≤20%; at risk 20–35%; off track >35%.
truth_meter.why: High thermal/other means the system still depends on non-wind, non-solar and unidentified supply.
truth_meter.logic: Calculated as demand not covered by detected wind, solar and net imports. It is not measured gas.

truth_meter.name: Grid carbon intensity
truth_meter.rule: On track ≤100 g/kWh; at risk 100–250; off track >250.
truth_meter.why: Carbon intensity indicates how clean the electricity actually is at the point of use.
truth_meter.logic: Lower values usually reflect stronger renewable output and/or lower fossil generation.

truth_meter.name: Energy affordability
truth_meter.rule: At risk if electricity >28 c/kWh or gas >10 c/kWh; off track if materially higher.
truth_meter.why: The transition remains politically fragile if households experience it mainly as cost pressure.
truth_meter.logic: Current household signals: electricity 31.72 c/kWh; gas 12.33 c/kWh.

truth_meter.name: EV transition
truth_meter.rule: On track only if uptake and fleet turnover continue to accelerate.
truth_meter.why: Transport electrification determines whether clean electricity can displace oil demand.
truth_meter.logic: New electric vehicle uptake is improving, but fleet turnover remains slower than headline sales suggest.

truth_meter.name: Heat transition
truth_meter.rule: At risk until heat-pump, retrofit and fossil-heating displacement signals are wired.
truth_meter.why: Heat is harder to decarbonise than electricity and needs its own evidence stream.
truth_meter.logic: Home heating is harder to decarbonise than electricity and needs separate public tracking.

truth_summary.method: Fixed three-label transition signal: on track, at risk, off track.
truth_summary.caveat: Signals combine live values, official annual indicators, computed proxies and labelled placeholders. Evidence basis is shown per module.

vocabulary.signal: A short interpretation of a reading.
vocabulary.verdict: Formal On track / At risk / Off track judgement. Used only in the Truth Meter.
vocabulary.diagnostic: Supporting explanation outside the Truth Meter.
vocabulary.evidence_basis: How current or authoritative the value is: Live, Official annual, Official semi-annual, Computed, Proxy, Placeholder or Unavailable.
vocabulary.gap: Always qualified: 2030 target gap, path gap, price gap or data gap.
vocabulary.residual_supply: Unclassified remainder after wind, solar and net imports. Not measured gas.
vocabulary.interconnection: Positive = importing, negative = exporting, near zero = near balanced.

metrics.label: Demand
metrics.caveat: Parsed from public Smart Grid Dashboard pages where available; spreadsheet fallback otherwise.

metrics.label: Renewables
metrics.definition: Wind plus solar as a share of current demand.
metrics.caveat: This is an operational wind+solar signal, not the official annual RES-E indicator.

metrics.label: Wind
metrics.definition: Wind generation as a share of current demand.
metrics.caveat: Weather-driven operational signal; not a policy-progress indicator on its own.

metrics.label: Solar
metrics.definition: Solar generation as a share of current demand.

metrics.label: Thermal/other
metrics.definition: Demand not covered by detected wind, solar and net imports.
metrics.caveat: Thermal/other is computed, not measured gas. It may include gas, hydro, storage, coal/oil or unidentified supply.

metrics.label: Interconnection
metrics.definition: Signed electricity exchange with neighbouring systems.
metrics.caveat: Positive means importing; negative means exporting. The public card reports direction rather than negative imports.

metrics.label: CO₂ intensity
metrics.definition: Carbon intensity of electricity generation/use signal.
metrics.caveat: Public Smart Grid Dashboard CO₂ signal; method depends on EirGrid published model.

metrics.label: 2030 target gap
metrics.definition: Gap between latest official annual RES-E value and the 80% 2030 benchmark.
metrics.caveat: Not a live grid value. It changes when official annual RES-E data update.

sections.purpose: Operational pulse: what the electricity system is doing now.
sections.method_note: Uses live and computed operational signals. Thirty-day lines are daily snapshots; estimated warm-start rows are visual scaffolding only.
Only this section uses formal verdicts: On track, At risk and Off track.
Uses annual RES-E progress against the 80% renewable-electricity benchmark.

sections.purpose: Distinguishes system market signals from household affordability.
sections.method_note: SEAI household prices are semi-annual official indicators, not live tariffs.
Schematic heatmap scaffold, not official county MWh. Intended to be replaced with SEAI county dashboard values for total MWh, MWh/km² and population-density normalisation.

electricity_metrics.caveat: Thermal/other is computed, not measured gas. It may include gas, hydro, storage, coal/oil or unidentified supply.

electricity_metrics.definition: Signed electricity exchange with neighbouring systems.

electricity_metrics.label: 2030 target gap
electricity_metrics.definition: Gap between latest official annual RES-E value and the 80% 2030 benchmark.
electricity_metrics.caveat: Not a live grid value. It changes when official annual RES-E data update.

target_drift.interpretation: Ireland reached 41.3% renewable electricity in 2024. To reach 80% by 2030, the remaining gap is 38.7 percentage points, requiring about 6.45 percentage points per year from 2024.
target_drift.caveat: This is an annual official RES-E indicator. It is not the same as the live quarter-hourly electricity mix shown in the Energy Now section.

prices.label: Household electricity
prices.detail: Latest official SEAI semester: 2025S1; up 1.13 c/kWh from previous period; 2.38 c/kWh above EU-27. Not a live supplier tariff.

prices.label: Household gas
prices.detail: Latest official SEAI semester: 2025S1; down 1.35 c/kWh from previous period; 0.65 c/kWh above EU-27. Not a live supplier tariff.

prices.label: Transport fuels
prices.detail: Transport-fuel price layer not yet wired. Planned after electricity and gas.

market_prices.label: Electricity system price
market_prices.freshness: latest settlement period visible on Smart Grid Dashboard
market_prices.source: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard imbalance settlement price
market_prices.detail: Latest electricity imbalance settlement price. This is a system/market signal, not a household electricity tariff.

market_prices.label: Gas balancing price
market_prices.freshness: manually entered from Gas Networks Ireland table/export
market_prices.detail: Gas Networks Ireland System Average Price / imbalance price. This is a gas-system balancing signal, not a household gas tariff. Value entered from official GNI table/export while the hidden endpoint is being wired.

stats.manual_note: Read from GNI imbalance prices table/export on 2026-05-13.

market_price_meta.caveat: Market/system prices are not household tariffs. Household affordability remains the SEAI semi-annual price layer.
sources: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard imbalance settlement price

gas.narrative: Uncovered demand is calculated from live demand minus mapped wind and solar cover. It is not measured gas. A later fuel-mix endpoint should split this into gas, hydro, storage, imports and other sources.

source_status.source: EirGrid System Data Qtr Hourly spreadsheet
source_status.caveat: Best-effort parser. Thermal/other is not pure gas.

smartgrid_live.source: EirGrid Smart Grid Dashboard public pages
smartgrid_live.caveat: Smart Grid visible-page parser is disabled for demand, wind and solar because visible-page scraping can confuse actual, forecast and chart values. Core grid quantities use the official EirGrid quarter-hourly workbook instead.

carbon_intensity.caveat: Parsed from Smart Grid Dashboard chart API. ROI is preferred; ALL is used only as fallback and labelled.

smartgrid_api_live.caveat: Uses latest non-null actual rows at or before Ireland local time. Future null rows are rejected.

smartgrid_visible_overview.source: SmartGrid Dashboard visible System Generation page
smartgrid_visible_overview.caveat: Uses the visible System Generation KPI values. Net Import % is converted to MW from visible system generation for the top KPI card.

current_electricity_normalisation.caveat: Generation mix is normalised so wind + solar + other renewables + thermal/other = 100. Interconnection MW is derived from visible SmartGrid net-import percentage and generation. Demand is withheld from top accounting cards when it fails the generation + net-flow balance check.

county_hosting.title: County Hosting Index
county_hosting.caveat: Schematic heatmap scaffold, not official county MWh. Intended to be replaced with SEAI county dashboard values for total MWh, MWh/km² and population-density normalisation.

method.display: Schematic tile heatmap, not boundary-accurate geography
method.next_step: Harvest or manually curate SEAI county MWh and normalised county metrics.

summary.spatial_question: Who hosts renewable infrastructure, who consumes electricity, and where is the transition uneven?
